Oxyanthus phragmites is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Africa, due to its health benefits which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxyanthus phragmites include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Oxyanthus phragmites include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and malaria.
